sentence_reviews.text: Behavior is learned from demonstrations, or the reward structure explaining demonstrations is inferred.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'; RL book discussion of imitation/apprenticeship and inverse reinforcement learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse RL from demonstrations is directly supported by AIMA's topic listing; imitation learning from demonstrations is supported by standard RL references but not directly visible in the inspected AIMA line.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' with 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning' under the same chapter.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse reinforcement learning is directly included under the reinforcement-learning chapter; imitation learning is not directly named in the inspected source.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' and 22.7 'Inverse Reinforcement Learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Inverse RL is explicitly presented as an RL topic; imitation learning is related through learning from demonstrations.
